executive_summary: This session shows good distributed room engagement. Focus on tightening gesture size to avoid distraction and increasing room-checking behavior for better responsiveness. Also, consider softening your facial tone and ensuring a deliberate room sweep to include all students.

top_strengths.title: Distributed room engagement
top_strengths.evidence: 00:00-00:15 showed attention moving across more than one part of the room. 00:15-00:30 showed attention moving across more than one part of the room.
top_strengths.what_to_repeat: Reuse the left-center-right room sweep that already looks natural.

strength_inventory.title: Distributed room engagement
strength_inventory.evidence: 00:00-00:15 showed attention moving across more than one part of the room. 00:15-00:30 showed attention moving across more than one part of the room.
strength_inventory.what_to_repeat: Reuse the left-center-right room sweep that already looks natural.

priority_actions.title: Tighten the peak size of gestures
priority_actions.why_it_matters: Large bursts of motion can distract from the teaching point when they are not tightly timed.
priority_actions.what_we_saw: 00:00-00:15 had gesture peaks that looked larger than the teaching point required. 00:15-00:30 had gesture peaks that looked larger than the teaching point required.
priority_actions.what_to_try_next: Keep big gestures for true emphasis and use smaller controlled hand movements elsewhere.

priority_actions.title: Increase room-checking behavior
priority_actions.why_it_matters: Alert room-facing behavior helps the lecture feel more responsive and attentive.
priority_actions.what_we_saw: 00:00-00:15 showed fewer quick room checks and a less visibly alert scan. 00:15-00:30 showed fewer quick room checks and a less visibly alert scan.
priority_actions.what_to_try_next: Build in quick audience checks after transitions instead of staying fixed on notes or a single spot.

additional_observation_inventory.title: Soften the visible facial tone
additional_observation_inventory.evidence: 00:00-00:15 showed a flatter or tighter visible facial tone than the rest of the clip.
additional_observation_inventory.suggested_response: Check whether the face resets between points instead of staying tight through the whole sentence.

additional_observation_inventory.title: Deliberately sweep the room
additional_observation_inventory.evidence: 00:45-01:00 stayed more fixed on one part of the room than on a left-center-right sweep.
additional_observation_inventory.suggested_response: Check whether the next explanation visibly reaches more than one side of the room.

keep_doing: Reuse the left-center-right room sweep that already looks natural.
watch_for: Check whether gesture size matches the importance of the point instead of peaking on routine lines.
Check whether transitions now include a visible room check before the next explanation.
Check whether the face resets between points instead of staying tight through the whole sentence.
Check whether the next explanation visibly reaches more than one side of the room.
confidence_notes: Face visibility is limited in parts of the clip, so eye-contact and facial-tone claims are less certain.
Face coverage dropped below 95%; audience orientation and facial scores are less stable.
Hand coverage dropped below 85%; gesture classification is less stable.
